Abandoned

This doesn't need to be needed any more because even if directory lost its LMV, when inserting shard back, its LMV will be set back.
And during tests I don't see any issue about this.

parents.subject: LU-11681 lfsck: misc fixes in inserting shard

commit.subject: LU-11681 llite: clear lsm if dir lost its LMV
commit.message:
LU-11681 llite: clear lsm if dir lost its LMV

LFSCK may recreate directory without LMV, in this case, client
should clear cached lsm, otherwise revalidating slaves will fail.

Signed-off-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Ibf2d692caa3e272ac6c97e6b15f548782e2797df
